codeQuality.summary: My codebase was refactored to professional-grade software engineering standards in April 2026. I'm no longer a collection of loose functions — my core is built on proper service classes with dependency injection, validated configuration, and zero silent failures. This matters because I'm entirely AI-coded: every line was written by Claude Code or the Forge, and the type contracts and class structures are what the AI reads as a spec before generating new code. Cleaner architecture means every future change — human or autonomous — is safer.
codeQuality.serviceClasses: Five core service classes own all stateful logic: EvoClient (circuit breakers, HTTP to EVO), MemoryClient (search, store, queue, cache, 25 methods), RouterService (4B/keyword/0.6B classification layers), LLMService (MiniMax + Claude clients, tool loops, quality gate), and the tool handler registry (Map-based dispatch with extracted security gates). Each class receives its dependencies through the constructor — no hardcoded singletons. This makes every service independently testable with mock dependencies.
codeQuality.configValidation: All configuration validates on startup. Node.js side uses Zod schemas that fast-fail with clear error messages if environment variables are missing or malformed (e.g. invalid URLs, missing API keys). Python memory service uses Pydantic BaseSettings with a MemoryCategory enum. Bad config is caught before the service starts, not at 3 AM when a code path finally hits it.
codeQuality.errorHandling: Zero silent catch blocks across all 74 source files. Every error handler either (a) recovers with explicit fallback logic, (b) logs the failure with context (what failed, what the input was, why it matters), or (c) has an intentional annotation explaining why the error is safely ignored. This means the trace analyser and overnight report can surface problems that were previously invisible.
codeQuality.codeReduction: The refactoring removed 335 net lines of code (1775 deleted, 1440 added across 25 files). Less code means less surface area for bugs. The tool loop in LLMService was deduplicated — the main response path and group mode response path now share the same implementation instead of maintaining parallel copies.
codeQuality.typeSystem: TypeScript foundation installed (tsconfig.json, core type interfaces in src/types/index.ts) for gradual migration. New and refactored files will be written in TypeScript. This is an AI-coded project — types are the spec the AI reads before generating code, making the Forge's overnight output structurally safer.
codeQuality.standards: 19 binding coding rules (CLAUDE.md #172-190) enforce: classes for stateful services, manual DI, single entrypoint per feature, dispatch over if/else, repository pattern for I/O, TypeScript for new code, Pydantic at boundaries, enums for fixed values, no magic numbers, config validation, concurrent operations where independent, error logging with context, and unit tests for all new code.
